programme.title: The David Rubenstein Show: Peer to Peer Conversations
programme.desc: Financier and philanthropist David Rubenstein talks with his peers -- leaders of some of America's biggest companies -- in this interview show that aims to answer the question of what makes a great leader. Rubenstein uses his chats to explore successful leadership skills through the personal and professional choices made by the business world's most influential people. Rubenstein travels across the country to find out about the business leaders' stories and uncover information about their paths to success. The interview subjects include Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates, Berkshire Hathaway chairman Warren Buffett, and Pepsi CEO Indra Nooyi.

programme.title: Bloomberg Markets: Asia
programme.desc: In an effort to provide extensive coverage of worldwide financial markets, Bloomberg features several regional shows under the "Bloomberg Markets" banner, with each focusing on that geographic area's markets. This Asian-focused version is hosted by Shery Ahn, David Ingles, Haidi Lun, Rishaad Salamat and Angie Lau. They report the latest business news and speak with the likes of political and business leaders to help investors make investment decisions that build their portfolios.

programme.title: Bloomberg Technology
programme.desc: Technology is not only becoming an increasingly important part of people's lives but also of the American economy. "Bloomberg Technology" takes a look at how the latest happenings in the tech industry are affecting the U.S. markets. Analysis, commentary, interviews and interactive graphics are among the staples of the show that help viewers understand the impact the technology sector has on the economy. The show, which was originally titled "Bloomberg West," is anchored by Emily Chang in San Francisco.

programme.title: East L.A. King (Español)
programme.desc: El líder de una pandilla en el este de Los Ángeles va a la cárcel después de un trato fallido. La prisión lo cambia, pero su hijo cae en pandillas.

programme.title: La Suburban de las Monjas
programme.desc: Cuatro hermosas monjas se dedican a conquistar a los hombres con sus sonrisas celestiales y siembran el terror con sus armas de alto poder.

programme.title: Misión extrajudicial
programme.desc: Sebastián Ornelas, además de ser el amo y señor de todo el poblado, es el hombre más buscado por las autoridades. Un día, su guapa mujer conoce a un judicial, quién está a cargo de su captura, y ahí comienza la verdadera cacería.

programme.title: Chicas bien
programme.desc: En un intento de pasar un buen rato y olvidar el posible divorcio de sus padres, Fernanda decide invitar a sus mejores amigas a pasar un fin de semana en su casa de campo.

programme.title: Monstruosamente solo
programme.desc: A un hombre le rompen el corazón y decide encerrarse en su departamento para estar solo, pero no puede evitar el apoyo de quienes lo quieren e incluso del monstruo imaginario de su infancia.

programme.title: Retrato familiar
programme.desc: Dos secuestradores irrumpen en la casa de una familia de la alta sociedad mexicana. Los criminales necesitan dinero para destinarlo a la consecución de una información que alguien les ha exigido.

programme.desc: Debido al fallecimiento de su madre, un famoso chef regresa de mala gana a su pequeña ciudad natal. Mientras redescubre su amor por la cocina, se reencuentra con un viejo amor que está a punto de casarse.

programme.title: Canon: fidelidad al límite
programme.desc: Mariana y Julián lo tienen todo: dos hijos, éxito y amor mutuo. Entonces intentan un matrimonio abierto, y la situación se pone fea.

programme.title: El triunfo del mojado
programme.desc: Pablo, un campesino, viaja a Estados Unidos en busca de una vida mejor. Pero el plan se complica cuando descubre que su familia ha sido secuestrada.

programme.title: Mi amor de las estrellas
programme.desc: Min-joon confronta a Jae-kyung sobre todas las vidas que ha destruido. Song-yi le dice a Min-joon que se aleje de ella, pero hace lo opuesto.

programme.title: Mi amor de las estrellas
programme.desc: Min-joon se desploma después de besar a Song-yi. Hwi-kyung se despierta y pretende que no sabe nada de los planes de Jae-kyung. Min-joon le dice a Song-yi que debe irse.

programme.title: Melissa
programme.desc: Reyhan y Mahinur van a la granja para hablar con Fatma. Metin defiende a Öykü y golpea a Siman. Orhan se enoja al saber que Meyati pagó dos millones más por la granja de Fatma.

programme.title: Melissa
programme.desc: Orhan le avisa a Cüneyt que acepta formar parte de la cooperativa. Selim acude a la inauguración de la fundación y sorprende a Busra. Orhan se entera de que el cantante no llegará a tiempo así que Kadir toma su lugar.

programme.title: Melissa
programme.desc: Sinan busca a Öyku y al ver que está con Metin, se presenta como su novio. Orhan y Busra se besan y Selim los ve. Öyku va a casa de Metin para disculparse, pero Mahinur es dura con ella y le pide alejarse.

programme.title: Melissa
programme.desc: Alguien arroja una piedra con un mensaje amenazante a la ventana de la casa de Yesim. Mahinur amenaza a Öykü. Selim intenta decirle la verdad a la madre de Busra. Besime alucinando con Sermin.

programme.title: Melissa
programme.desc: Metin encuentra una nota de Öykü en el invernadero. Los niños del colegio piden autógrafos a Kadir después de la fiesta. Öykü está triste porque se imagina que Metin no la ha perdonado.

programme.title: Señora del Destino
programme.desc: María do Carmo es una madre de cinco hijos que triunfó en la vida con mucha lucha y tesón, pero que todavía tendrá una batalla que luchar: obtener el cariño de su hija quien fue secuestrada cuando era una recién nacida.

programme.title: Señora del Destino

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Para protegerlo, Jade toma aire y fuerzas para decirle a Lucas que no lo ama y lo echa de la casa. Ella se siente destruida y, con más fastidio hacia Said, aplica el plan de aburrirlo.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Jade no se rinde y le quema la ropa a Said, pero él ya se dio cuenta de sus intenciones de desesperarlo y le grita que jamás le va a dar el divorcio. Ella le suplica, pero no consigue nada.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Con dolor y resignación, Lucas acepta su destino con Marisa y el bebé que van a tener. Jade se entera de que Lucas va a ser padre y rompe en llanto. Cristina averigua por el papá del niño de Dora.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Jade quiere otra esposa para Said y habla con Muna, una mujer bella y soltera, le dice que Said busca otra mujer. Cristina invita a Leonardo a la fiesta de Daniel, Albieri está nervioso.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: En cuanto ve a su hija, Lucas se conmueve y olvida todas sus discusiones con Marisa. Albieri inventa otra mentira, dice que el embrión fue implantado en otro vientre, no en el de Dora.

programme.title: La esclava Isaura
programme.desc: Una historia que se desenvuelve a mediados del siglo XIX en Brasil y relata la vida de Isaura, una esclava mestiza, hija de la esclava del Comendador y el capataz de la hacienda.

programme.title: La esclava Isaura

programme.title: Génesis
programme.desc: Asenat quiere entregarse a Teruel. El arquero es asesinado. Merianath es operada por la herida de flecha. Sumido en su dolor, Israel le exige a Rubén buscar el cuerpo de José. Judá está arrepentido de lo que hicieron a José y suspende la búsqueda.

programme.title: Moisés y los diez mandamientos
programme.desc: La historia de Moisés desde su nacimiento y llegada a manos de la princesa Henutmire hasta llevar a su pueblo a la Tierra Prometida. Su crianza como un verdadero príncipe egipcio junto a su tío Ramsés.

programme.title: El laberinto de Alicia
programme.desc: Alicia Vega, psicóloga del colegio Los Ángeles, especialista en prevención y tratamiento de abusos sexuales a menores, inicia una investigación cuando Valentina, una niña adorable y frágil, parece estar en la mira de un abusador.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Nazira se presta al juego, anima a Muna, promete enseñarle todo para que Said se quede con ella y se divorcie de Jade, no lo consigue. Leonardo y Cristina hacen el amor, él se siente culpable.

programme.title: Mi amor de las estrellas
programme.desc: Song-yi cuestiona la necesidad de Min-joon de volver a su planeta. Min-joon, al igual que Song-yi, se siente angustiado ante la perspectiva. Los poderes de Min-joon siguen fallando.

programme.title: Génesis
programme.desc: Adaptación de uno de los libros más reconocidos de la Biblia, que aborda la historia del origen de la humanidad, invitando a entender el presente, volviendo al principio de todo. La caída de Lucifer, la vida de Adán y Eva, Noé y el diluvio, y otros.

programme.desc: Chefs recreate each other’s signature dish, but they can only determine what the dish is using their senses of taste, touch and smell. When the masks come off, the heat is on, with each chef attempting to cook their opponent’s creation.

programme.title: West By Orphan Train
programme.desc: A gripping documentary uncovering real stories of children loaded on trains, sent west, and presented “for the picking.” From 1854 to 1929, over 6,000 children landed in Iowa communities. Discover their emotional journeys and impact on the Midwest.

programme.title: New Hampshire Chronicle
programme.desc: New Hampshire Chronicle highlights the people and places that make the Granite State so special. Presented by WMUR.

programme.title: In Transit by Esquire
programme.desc: Host Dave Holmes meets celebrity guests at the airport bar to discuss must-do activities in cities across America. So when you’re ready to board your next flight, you’ll have an Esquire-approved itinerary.

programme.title: Silver Linings
programme.desc: Welcome to Lancaster, PA, one of the country's hottest retirement hubs. Here, seniors, from a beauty pageant queen to a 90 year-old swim champion aren’t slowing down; they’re rewriting the playbook and redefining aging in America.

programme.title: WCVB Local News
programme.desc: Get the latest news and weather from WCVB.

programme.title: LIVE: On The Record
programme.desc: Massachusetts lawmakers and newsmakers go “On The Record” every Sunday with NewsCenter 5 and a political roundtable reviews the top political news of the week.

programme.title: LIVE: CityLine
programme.desc: CityLine5 is WCVB's award-winning urban news and feature magazine talk show.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: If you’re craving gingerbread, Christmas cookies, chocolate babka, or even gluten-free holiday treats, tonight we have you covered.

programme.title: Wellness Hour: Restoring Your Smile
programme.desc: The Benefits Of Dental Implants.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Think lobsters are pricey? They’ve got nothing on “glass eels,” now a beyond-booming industry on the coast of Maine. We visit Ellsworth, where at today’s price of $2,200 a pound, there’s heavy competition to net the tiny, wriggling baby eels for export to Asia. We meet some of the lucky few licensed in the glass eel trade, including the so-called “godfather” of the industry, as well as ordinary Mainers whose fortunes have changed thanks to a coveted license. And we talk to Maine law enforcement about the early days of the eel trade, a wild-west scene replete with guns, cash – and eels.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Those topics that just might be off the table in your house, or the ones that make you turn just a little bit red. Tonight, Chronicle is pulling back the curtain on some “taboo topics” - from politics at the dinner table to trips to the restroom.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: From nostalgic balsa wood airplanes, to sophisticated space suits made in Worcester – including the suit Sunita Williams wore on her most recent and much-longer-than-expected visit to space. Tonight Nicole Estaphan brings us all things “sky-high”.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: New Englanders love spending time by the water, even in winter. Anthony Everett visits Chatham, on Cape Cod, where he meets a business exec turned fine art photographer, chronicles the rebirth of a local movie theater, and grabs a bite at…the airport? Shayna Seymour visits coastal Freeport, Maine – during a snowstorm!

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: They call it the “Bidd-aissance” – the rebirth underway in Biddeford, Maine. Tonight Anthony Everett heads to the former mill town, now becoming a foodie mecca. Finally, Shayna Seymour is exploring Westerly, R.I., where she gets spooked on a ghost tour, shops for books, and overnights at the historic Weekapaug Inn.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight Shayna Seymour heads to Lake Sunapee in New Hampshire, where the ice is out, a local farm is bursting with life, and the community is gearing up for peak tourist season.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Nicole Estaphan heads north to explore lake life in Naples, Maine, where she hops aboard a paddlewheeler on Long Lake; stumbles across Shaquille O’Neal’s sneaker at a unique shop; and bites into a “Sebagel” at a local bagel shop

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: They call it the “Bidd-aissance” – the rebirth underway in Biddeford, Maine. Tonight Anthony Everett heads to the former mill town, now fast becoming a foodie mecca. He samples local seafood; visits historic City Theater; and checks out the city’s hip new hotel. Erika Tarantal heads south to Marion, Mass., stopping in its quaint downtown; exploring the local natural history museum and a historic public golf course; and sampling the local catch. Finally, Shayna Seymour is exploring Westerly, R.I., where she gets spooked on a ghost tour, shops for books, and overnights at the historic Weekapaug Inn.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Anthony Everett heads to midcoast Maine, where in Rockport he discovers a tiny bookshop with an inspiring origin story; encounters some special cows at a working farm/nature preserve; and takes in the ocean views from the classic Samoset Resort.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Anthony Everett uses a tank and a ferry to get to this summer destination – Block Island. He gets a taste of summer life on the Block, hits up local restaurant The Oar and the revamped Block Island Beach House, and meets the gifted artist behind the charming “glass float” project. Meanwhile, Shayna Seymour pays a summer visit to ever-charming Mystic, Conn. She frolics with the sea life at Mystic Aquarium, travels back in time at Mystic Seaport Museum, makes a quick stop at Mystic Pizza (naturally) and checks out the charming Whalers Inn.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight Erika Tarantal hits Providence, R.I., where she checks out the art scene, tours a historic hotel, and even takes a blacksmithing class. While Shayna Seymour visits Portsmouth, N.H and Nicole Estaphan goes to Portland, Maine.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: We’re headed north, south, and east for these summer escapes.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight we visit coastal communities that are waking up from their winter slumber. Shayna Seymour heads north to Kennebunkport, Maine, where she samples ice cream with outrageous flavors at Rococo Dessert Bar, visits an artisan boutique, and beds down at the historic Kennebunkport Inn.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: If winter has you chilled to the bone, we have ideas to warm you up! Erika Tarantal is “skinning” up Black Mountain in Jackson, N.H., then warming up at the Villa Hygge Hotel in North Conway, where she introduces us to the cozy Scandinavian concept of “hygge.” Shayna Seymour heads to Vermont’s Mad River Valley, where diners at the original American Flatbread can dine fireside in their own private cabins. And Nicole Estaphan dines in an igloo in Portland, Maine, and visits a hotel in nearby Cape Elizabeth, where the stories of dog rescues will warm the heart.

programme.title: Wellness Hour: Restoring Your Smile
programme.desc: The Benefits Of Dental Implants.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Shayna Seymour is heading up to York, Maine to experience a slice of this summertime haven at the height of fall. From a cliff-side resort to a brewery steeped in family tradition - Shayna discovers why fall is perfect along the Maine coast.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Forget the jingle bells and candy canes – New Englanders have some unusual holiday habits!

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: LIVE: SportsCenter 5 OT
programme.desc: The latest scores, updates, and analysis from the WCVB Sports Team.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: It’s not just “Dry January.” Polling indicates more Americans are tempering their alcohol intake, and tonight we talk to consumers, businesses, and experts about the changes.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Jamaica Plain is constantly evolving but one constant is Centre Street. We make the rounds of Boston’s official “Latin Quarter” and talk with residents, business owners, and activists about what’s changed and what hasn’t - namely, its deep sense of community.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Ted Reinstein is back with all new stories of New Englanders following their passions.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: We’re visiting “Mill” towns tonight -- that’s, towns that begin with M-I-L-L (sorry, Milton and Milford!). There are only three “Mill-,” towns in Massachusetts and Ted Reinstein is exploring them all. In Millis, Ted discovers a wine shop where one can find the perfect bottle to pair with anything from fine dining to “ketchup-smothered food.” Then, he is off to Millbury for a tour of the historic Asa Waters Mansion. He also meets a local cake decorator whose designs may be “too hot for TV.” Finally, in Millville, Ted checks in with the Millville High School band marching toward another state title.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight Ted Reinstein explores a snowy stretch of highway between Vermont’s largest city (Burlington) and its capital city (Montpelier). In Richmond he visits a community ski hill that has bred Olympians, and the town’s rather unusual round landmark.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: From Saratoga Springs to West Port, find out where adventures await just a ‘Tank Away.’

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: Bringing Down The House: The Bombing of Harvey's Casino
programme.desc: In the summer of 1980, two men wheeled what looked like an IBM computer into a Lake Tahoe casino. The events that followed rocked the industry, baffled bomb technicians, and changed the lives of a criminal’s and a casino magnate’s family forever.

programme.title: The Lost Children of Carlisle
programme.desc: A powerful look at the history of Native American boarding schools as told through the stories of tribal members and their surviving family members, who endured an educational system founded on the concept “Kill the Indian in him, and save the man.”

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Anthony Everett heads to the heart of New Hampshire’s Monadnock region to visit the often-overlooked town of Hancock, where he meets the artist behind curious woodland creatures, and visits the longest continuously-operating inn in New Hampshire. Nicole Estaphan keeps the adventure rolling in Quechee, Vermont with a Segway tour guide who doubles as the local Toy Museum curator and collector. Meanwhile, Shayna Seymour visits Rhode Island’s largest greenhouse at Clark Farms, dines in a “hobbit house,” and while in South County visits with new café owners Ashley and Jared, aka the “most dramatic” couple from ABC’s “Bachelor in Paradise,” who are now in the restaurant biz.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Iconic, aluminum travel trailers sound like a throwback, and they are. But at an Airstream “campground” on Cape Cod, Erika Tarantal discovers this retro vehicle has undergone some modern upgrades. Nicole Estaphan spends the night in a refurbished 100-year-old train caboose in Chester, Mass. And once you crash at one vehicle-turned-hotel, why not try another? Nicole spends the night on a 43-foot motor yacht moored in Charlestown. Join us as we check-in to some “Rare” BnBs.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight we take a trip to Eliot, Maine, where we check out Sandy Hill Farm, an outdoor light show. A couple of hours to the west and north we explore Hanover, New Hampshire a charming community that is a hub of culture, dining, and outdoor activity.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: You’ve heard his name… but maybe not realized his impact. Frederick Law Olmsted’s landscape designs include New York’s Central Park, the U.S. Capitol grounds, and of course, Boston’s Emerald Necklace. In honor of Olmsted’s 200th birthday we’re celebrating his achievements – visiting his home studio in Brookline and touring a private, Olmsted-designed estate that has been perfectly preserved. We also learn about Olmsted’s belief that parks are essential to democracy, and should always be free and open to all.

programme.title: FIXT
programme.desc: Andrew returned home from serving his country with a toothache so bad he covers his mouth out of habit. Mickey had forgotten how to smile after years of struggle with addiction and abuse. Will the FIXT team help them smile again?

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Exploring Boston’s Historic 7-mile street, ‘Adams Street.’

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: WCVB

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: In January 2022 the City of Boston dismantled a tent encampment in a neighborhood plagued by illicit drug use and violence. So…what happened after that? We meet people who have resettled in transitional housing, get a look inside the cottages where some now receive services, and ride along with city outreach workers trying to serve those who are still struggling at Mass and Cass. We also talk to business owners and city officials trying to avoid a return to the dangerous status quo and devise a long-term solution to the crisis.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: In July, catastrophic floods brought parts of Vermont to its knees. Farms in western Massachusetts suffered, too. Beginning immediately after the floods, Chronicle began talking with Vermonters whose homes, businesses, and communities were devastated by this shock from Mother Nature. From Barre, Montpelier, and Ludlow, Vt., we bring you their stories – and their optimism about the future (leaf peepers welcome!). We also talk to Massachusetts farmers whose crops were wiped out, to find out how they’re rebuilding.

programme.title: FIXT
programme.desc: Andrew returned home from serving his country with a toothache so bad he covers his mouth out of habit. Mickey had forgotten how to smile after years of struggle with addiction and abuse. Will the FIXT team help them smile again?

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Dinner in a treehouse, or under a tree? Tonight we try both, as we head out in search of unique spots to enjoy a meal al fresco.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Explore the Main streets and backroads of Amherst, MA.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: As he does every year, Anthony Everett spent time on the water in 2021. This year, that time also included joining Coast Guard officers training for search and rescue missions. He also drove a military tank and reported on the growing division and spread of misinformation in our country. Tonight, Anthony looks back at his favorite stories of 2021.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: Unbelievable
programme.desc: Brace yourself for heart-stopping heroics as rescuers leap into action! A semi-truck teeters dangerously over the Ohio River, a devastating gas explosion reduces homes to rubble, and a capsized cargo ship leaves its crew fighting for survival.

programme.title: Stitch
programme.desc: From craftsmen to seamstresses, chefs to dancers, people are turning their hobbies into full-time hustles. Find out how these innovative Americans lean into what they love to reinvent their careers and give back to their communities.

programme.title: Maker Nation
programme.desc: Beach-inspired resin art in Boston, and elaborate charcuterie boards in Omaha.

programme.title: Show Me Milwaukee
programme.desc: Join fashion icon Christopher DeAngelo Gilbert as he takes Greg on a creative adventure. Together they dance, get styled at a unique tailor and hat maker, create a memorable dinner, and improv with Milwaukee's next generation of actors.

programme.title: What's On The Menu?
programme.desc: Join us as we skip the line of Greenville's most sought after weekend eateries for brunch. We'll head to Southern Culture and enjoy music with our grits, check out the Bohemian's DIY Bloody Mary Bar, and round out the day with Biscuit Head and Hen Dough.

programme.title: Local Love
programme.desc: Sommer is a lawyer who’s been struggling to find a suitable mate in Kansas City. Three lucky singles will try their luck as they take her to the driving range, yoga and meditation at a studio and on a whiskey distillery tour at J. Rieger & Co.

programme.title: Finding Adventure
programme.desc: Ashley, 28, has spent 14 years raising her three children. Kinga comes to Manchester to get Ashley out into nature for the first time so she can share these experiences and pass them along to her children.

programme.title: Eat Play Stay
programme.desc: Discover where they serve the best omelets on the planet in ‘Biggest Little City in the World.’ Meet an unlikely taxidermist, open a secret door to a room filled with booze, and sample the cuisine of other cultures by eating hot dogs.

programme.title: Searching for Johnny
programme.desc: (2022) The disappearance of Johnny Gosch is one of Iowa's longest running, and most notorious mysteries. In 1982, the 12-year-old paperboy in West Des Moines vanished without a trace. Forty years later, KCCI uncovers where the investigation stands.

programme.title: Sports Town Stories
programme.desc: From a popular new pro volleyball team drawing huge crowds, to a former football pro who now coaches e-sports, these Nebraskan athletes show how to seize the moment.

programme.title: On The Job
programme.desc: Brendan goes underwater with dolphins and sting rays to learn about ocean conservation, tests his construction skills against a LEGO Master Builder, and preps a theme park dedicated to one of children's most beloved TV characters, Peppa Pig!

programme.title: Weathering the Storm
programme.desc: Hurricane Ian unleashed catastrophic destruction across Southwest Florida, washing entire neighborhoods to sea. As loved ones are separated and communities face unimaginable loss, extraordinary acts of courage emerge in the storm’s aftermath.

programme.title: Ballin' in Baltimore
programme.desc: Angel gears up for a day of nonstop action with adrenaline-junkie and restaurateur, Steve Chu. They get their hearts pumping with crazy-fast jet skis, hardcore jiu jitsu training, a sky-high ropes course and an epic paintball showdown.

programme.title: Weathering the Storm
programme.desc: On December 10, 2021, a violent tornado outbreak hit western Kentucky. It was the deadliest weather event in the history of the Commonwealth. But as families faced a bleak holiday season, hope emerged in surprising ways.

programme.title: Hit Me With Your Best Spot
programme.desc: With the help of notable Kansas City locals Matt Besler, Kemet Coleman, and DJs Ice Kole & Joe, we discover a silent disco aboard a KC streetcar, explore The French Market, and make ourselves at home amongst the stars at Garozzo's Italian tavern.

programme.title: FIXT
programme.desc: Andrew returned home from serving his country with a toothache so bad he covers his mouth out of habit. Mickey had forgotten how to smile after years of struggle with addiction and abuse. Will the FIXT team help them smile again?

programme.title: Eat Play Stay
programme.desc: Relive the '50s, sip smokin’ cocktails, and discover salty licorice fish in Amish country's hidden gem.

programme.title: Chaos & Kindness
programme.desc: Justin and Ryan take you backstage and give a unique view of their winter tour in New Hampshire. See what it takes to make their show a smashing success!

programme.title: In Transit by Esquire
programme.desc: Esquire’s Dave Holmes chats with Gaten Matarazzo from Stranger Things and shares an Esquire-approved itinerary for your next trip to Boston.

programme.title: Blind Kitchen
programme.desc: It’s a culinary battle of the senses between two iconic chefs in the City Beautiful. Watch as Chef Liz of Garp & Fuss in Winter Park takes on Chef Trina of Se7en Bites in The Milk District. It’s The Knife versus The Whisk!

programme.title: Eat Play Stay
programme.desc: Rock Hill, South Carolina is chock full of adventure. From zip-lining through the trees to wake boarding with a pro. You can also admire beautiful murals by local artists, visit hard cider orchards and get snuggly in a romantic lakeside cabin.

programme.title: Bourbon Blaze: The Night That Tested America's Spirit
programme.desc: When a massive fire hits one of the nation’s largest bourbon distilleries, the families of competing bourbon companies have to decide whether to lend a hand… or let it burn. This is the story of bourbon, America’s native spirit.

programme.title: Weathering the Storm
programme.desc: Hurricane Ian unleashed catastrophic destruction across Southwest Florida, washing entire neighborhoods to sea. As loved ones are separated and communities face unimaginable loss, extraordinary acts of courage emerge in the storm’s aftermath.

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: In Milwaukee, 19-year-old Sade Robinson has gone missing, and her car is found torched. A trail of evidence across the city shocks the community and leaves her family searching for justice.

programme.title: Plate It!
programme.desc: This is the formal food frenzy you don't want to miss. Chefs Tyler Johnson and Ben Lefenfeld are getting bougie for this Black Tie Dinner challenge. Whose meal will win best dressed?

programme.title: Iconic Eats
programme.desc: Watch as Tess eats 21 iconic Disney World Animal Kingdom treats

programme.title: Local Love
programme.desc: Lifestyle blogger Morgan is ready to find her special someone. Three Des Moines bachelors try to capture her heart one date at a time. Will it be by getting creative, using a good offense or will she roll into love on wheels?

programme.title: Ballin' in Baltimore
programme.desc: Angel gets her sea legs with Maritime mogul, Captain Bobby LaPin. They sail Baltimore's iconic harbor, dive deep into the city's green initiatives, shuck oysters, and head out crabbing with the best seafarers Charm City has to offer.

programme.title: Free Spirit
programme.desc: Kate regains her faith in the classic Hurricane, talks history and equity in the cocktail industry, pledges allegiance to the French 75, and dives into a rich New Orleans cocktail culture that’s much bigger than Bourbon Street.

programme.title: Ed & Day in The 'Burgh
programme.desc: Ed and Day explore the local bike scene, learn to dance salsa, and get quizzed on their beer knowledge at Two Frays Brewery in Garfield.

programme.title: Crimes, Cons & Capers
programme.desc: Sometimes the bad guys are busted by regular people! These stories showcase good Samaritans stopping suspects in their tracks.

programme.title: Plate It!
programme.desc: Food truck father Jimmy Longoria is up against munchy meal mother Helen Markatos. Can these chefs elevate the kids menu? Or will their dishes send them to time out?

programme.title: Wait, What Happened?
programme.desc: Frank Nicotero is in Cincinnati asking locals questions about wild and wacky news stories that include some unbelievable weddings, kissing creatures and a swimming pool surprise.

programme.title: Local Love
programme.desc: Luke is a Kansas City bachelor who is finally ready to settle down. He’s purging the dating apps and hoping to find the perfect match. He’ll meet three blind dates as they play pickleball, get their shuffleboard on and visit a llama farm.

programme.title: Human Race by Runner's World
programme.desc: Runner-in-Chief, Jeff Dengate spotlights inspiring runners making a difference.

programme.title: Boston Rob Does Beantown
programme.desc: From meditating in a salt cave to braving a cold plunge, Rob treats his mind, body and soul to a variety of holistic therapies.

programme.title: Heroes On Call
programme.desc: In the 1960s, Americans were dying at shocking rates due to inadequate emergency care. Enter Freedom House Ambulance Service: the first paramedics unit in the U.S., run and staffed by Black men and women. This is the origin story of EMS.

programme.title: Stitch
programme.desc: Explore how designers blend heritage and innovation in fashion. From cultural celebrations to empowering future generations, their creative visions sew a new tomorrow.

programme.title: Maker Nation Challenge
programme.desc: Rain and chalk artists don't mix – and three Kansas City artists will not only have to take on the clock but the weather as they compete against each other to create art inspired by jazz – but how long can the dry pavement last?

programme.title: Maker Nation
programme.desc: A festive headpiece in New Orleans, and wooden toy making in Omaha.

programme.title: Americas Most Delish
programme.desc: Dive into the Midwest's fried delights. From Friday night fish fries to tasty testicles and fried grilled cheese? Enjoy the area's most iconic and delectable crispy fried goodness.

programme.title: Completed Episodes
programme.desc: Scott's life revolves around everyone's favorite four-legged friends, from rescuing and caring for countless dogs, his spirit is unwavering. Today Meaghan gives back a day full of moments for him and his beloved canine companion.

programme.title: Completed Episodes
programme.desc: Scott's life revolves around everyone's favorite four-legged friends, from rescuing and caring for countless dogs, his spirit is unwavering. Today Meaghan gives back a day full of moments for him and his beloved canine companion.

programme.title: Blind Kitchen
programme.desc: Puerto Rican Chefs Tanya Nicole of Lowell and Chef Calixto David from Chelsea spice it up in this Caribbean cook-off. Can Cali recreate a traditional dish flawlessly? Or will Tanya taste victory with a dish she’s never cooked before?

programme.title: Boston Rob Does Beantown
programme.desc: Rob takes the plunge with both old and new water-based activities, including paddleboard yoga, snowmobiling on the water, scuba diving and sailing.

programme.title: Ed & Day in The 'Burgh
programme.desc: Ed & Day reconnect with their youth, work on their flexibility in an artistic way, and explore local beer culture at Allegheny City Brewing in the Northside.

programme.title: Crimes, Cons & Capers
programme.desc: Whether it’s gathering thousands of mysterious, colorful packets stuffed in a store’s ceiling or valuable wildlife slithering to freedom, investigators must be ready to decisively face a range of mammoth tasks in the field.

programme.title: One Pill Can Kill
programme.desc: Told through the voices of grieving parents, this documentary follows the real and lasting impact of the fentanyl crisis. Capturing the aftermath of sudden loss, the search for answers, and the transformation of grief into public advocacy.

programme.title: Stitch
programme.desc: From inventors to marathon runners, interesting people are all over the country.

programme.title: Weathering the Storm
programme.desc: Hurricane Ian unleashed catastrophic destruction across Southwest Florida, washing entire neighborhoods to sea. As loved ones are separated and communities face unimaginable loss, extraordinary acts of courage emerge in the storm’s aftermath.

programme.title: In Transit by Esquire
programme.desc: Esquire’s Dave Holmes chats with Gaten Matarazzo from Stranger Things and shares an Esquire-approved itinerary for your next trip to Boston.

programme.title: Local Love
programme.desc: Austin is a bay area native who has never been in committed relationship. He’s in luck, as three Sacramento single guys vie for his affection taking him go kart racing, horseback riding and axe throwing.

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: The brutal murders of two trans women of color just 48 hours apart in New Orleans unsettle the community, leaving many wondering if the cases are connected. Detectives uncover clues, yet justice remains elusive.

programme.title: The Outcome is Income
programme.desc: The Outcome is Income

programme.title: Maker Nation Challenge
programme.desc: Three jewelry makers in Greenville are about to take on some heavy metal as they go head-to-head to design and build an arm cuff inspired by the Liberty Bridge. Will their designs be too arm-twisting for the judges?

programme.title: Maker Nation
programme.desc: Mardi Gras beading in New Orleans and a paper-folding artist in Austin.

programme.title: Chaos & Kindness
programme.desc: Darlene, a young superfan with cerebral palsy, becomes Justin's date for one magical night after the team hears her story and learns how much she loves the show.

programme.title: The Spark
programme.desc: From Naval helicopter pilot to fantasy costume designer, Zan Campbell’s life has been an adventure. Learn about his journey to find purpose and inspire joy in others.

programme.title: LIVE: WESH 2 News at 6pm
programme.desc: Details affecting local, regional and national news events of the day are provided by the WESH 2 Evening News Team, as well as updates on weather and traffic.

programme.title: Wait, What Happened?
programme.desc: What strange snack did a woman eat at a football game? What ice cream flavor was inspired by a hot dog topping? Frank Nicotero is asking these questions and more, as Cincinnati locals try to win cash prizes.

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: A beloved University of Kentucky football player is murdered on the porch of his house during his 21st birthday party in an assassination-style killing. What leads to the shooter's capture is equally shocking.

programme.title: Weathering the Storm
programme.desc: Hurricane Ian unleashed catastrophic destruction across Southwest Florida, washing entire neighborhoods to sea. As loved ones are separated and communities face unimaginable loss, extraordinary acts of courage emerge in the storm’s aftermath.

programme.title: Finding Adventure
programme.desc: Anthony Carignan is a bookish 26-year-old who has never felt at ease in the outdoors. Kinga has come to Maine to help Anthony connect with nature and live out his dream of becoming an authentic Mainer.

programme.title: Blind Kitchen
programme.desc: In the battle that could ruin their friendship, Chef Lexi Gawlak of Swine & Sons in Winter Park faces off with Chef Jes Tantalo of Redlight Redlight in Audubon Park. It's a tastebud brawl on this episode of Blind Kitchen.

programme.title: Eat Play Stay
programme.desc: Explore space travel and ale trails across the fun-filled land of Huntsville.

programme.title: Hit Me With Your Best Spot
programme.desc: Our local experts know the best thrills at Fun Spot. Orlando’s top comedian braves the gravity-defying SkyCoaster, a monster DJ and actor can’t stop screaming on White Lightning, and an influencer leaves all her followers behind on the go-kart track.

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: Two serial killers whose chilling crimes left their communities devastated.

programme.title: Crimes, Cons & Capers
programme.desc: These criminals may not have blood on their hands, but they still leave despair in their wake. From shady non-profits to fraudulent contractors, to wide-spread postal theft, here are some of the most devastating white-collar crimes to be attempted across the country.

programme.title: The Middle Child
programme.desc: In the 1980s, a serial killer buried a woman and three girls in the woods of New Hampshire. One of those girls is still unidentified. WMUR joins the investigators who promise they won’t give up until her name is restored.

programme.title: Witness to History
programme.desc: 20 years ago the Space Shuttle Columbia’s return to earth turned from celebration to sorrow. 7 astronauts and the first Shuttle to ever fly lost in a terrible accident. WESH 2 is opening the archives and sitting down with the veteran journalists who reported on the mission from triumph to tragedy.

programme.title: What's On The Menu?
programme.desc: There's nothing like a good sandwich. From smoked salmon, to build-your-own burgers, Baltimore's handhelds game is on point.

programme.title: Wait, What Happened?
programme.desc: Host Frank Nicotero visits "The Big Easy" armed with trivia questions about the unbelievable things people have seen in a bottle, under a church floorboard and even inside a mail truck!

programme.title: Americas Most Delish
programme.desc: Celebrate some of the Midwest's holiday favorites. Where scotcheroos take center stage, snicker salads delight, and the pink stuff surprises the pallet.

programme.title: Show Me Milwaukee
programme.desc: Milwaukee was built on a foundation of social clubs and communal effort. Former Emmy Award winning WISN reporter Shar Borg shows Greg her favorite community building businesses and activities.

programme.title: Plate It!
programme.desc: Let's "MEAT" today's chefs: BBQ Boss Chef Sam is looking to roast her competitor, Rotisserie Chef Joseph Burton, in today's game-day feast challenge. Which of these meat maestros will craft the ultimate game day spread?

programme.title: Boston Rob Does Beantown
programme.desc: What’s vast, adventure-packed, and sunscreen-free? Boston’s great indoors! Rob tries his hand at everything from indoor surfing to skydiving on his day in.

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy

programme.title: KCRA Local News
programme.desc: Get the latest news and weather from KCRA.

programme.title: Only In
programme.desc: Where can you find a book about choosing weird, a torch-wielding craftswoman, and a family bakery burning the midnight oil? The same place where alleyways have meaningful names and the American River changed the course of triathlons.

programme.title: Defying Diagnosis
programme.desc: One man's story of strength, love and support as he beats the odds fighting brain cancer.

programme.title: Farm to Fork
programme.desc: How cities are harnessing the power of agriculture to make real differences in the health and happiness of community members.

programme.title: Fighting for Freedom: The United States v. Hamid Hayat
programme.desc: 14 years after he was arrested on federal terrorism charges, a Northern California man is freed after his case is overturned.

programme.title: Dying to Ask
programme.desc: Stressed out? Try drinking a glass of water. A recent article in Women's Health broke down a study on the effects of being under-hydrated on anxiety and future health. Abigail Cuffey is the executive editor of Women's Health.

programme.title: Maker Nation Challenge
programme.desc: Rain and chalk artists don't mix – and three Kansas City artists will not only have to take on the clock but the weather as they compete against each other to create art inspired by jazz – but how long can the dry pavement last?

programme.title: Maker Nation
programme.desc: A Pittsburgh blacksmith, and an acrylic pour-artist in Boston.

programme.title: Weathering the Storm
programme.desc: As flames from the deadly 2017 Tubbs Fire tore through Santa Rosa, a first-time mother went into labor, while families fled with moments to spare… before making the powerful choice to return.

programme.title: Blind Kitchen
programme.desc: Years ago Chef Bergen Carmen from Meril and Chef Aaron Villeda from Bao Mi worked side-by-side; now they’re back getting their hands dirty for some friendly competition. Will his old pal chicken liver turn out to be Chef Villeda’s ultimate downfall?

programme.title: Breaking Bread
programme.desc: Dig into the famous farms behind Sacramento’s status as America’s farm-to-fork capital. We’ll travel from the kitchen to the land, exploring olive and walnut farms with the visionary chefs hand-sourcing ingredients for inspired dishes.

programme.title: Wait, What Happened?
programme.desc: What strange snack did a woman eat at a football game? What ice cream flavor was inspired by a hot dog topping? Frank Nicotero is asking these questions and more, as Cincinnati locals try to win cash prizes.

programme.title: Finding Adventure
programme.desc: Anthony Carignan is a bookish 26-year-old who has never felt at ease in the outdoors. Kinga has come to Maine to help Anthony connect with nature and live out his dream of becoming an authentic Mainer.

programme.title: One Pill Can Kill
programme.desc: Told through the voices of grieving parents, this documentary follows the real and lasting impact of the fentanyl crisis. Capturing the aftermath of sudden loss, the search for answers, and the transformation of grief into public advocacy.

programme.title: Completed Episodes
programme.desc: From a para track and field star, to members of an elite octogenarian rowing team and a once-in-a-generation female quarterback, these groundbreaking athletes are 'Boston Strong.'

programme.title: Human Race by Runner's World
programme.desc: Runner-in-Chief, Jeff Dengate, presents stories from around the country of runners using their sport for good.

programme.title: Defying Diagnosis
programme.desc: One man's story of strength, love and support as he beats the odds fighting brain cancer.

programme.title: Selling Citizenship
programme.desc: Selling Citizenship is a KCRA 3 documentary featuring interviews with the people who investigated, prosecuted, and covered the story for KCRA 3, following the circuitous and sometimes unbelievable story of a man whose very conviction ended up at the U.S. Supreme Court.

programme.title: Chaos & Kindness
programme.desc: Justin, Ryan and Tony introduce us to a real-life superhero who has opened her home and heart to special needs children.

programme.title: Completed Episodes
programme.desc: Frank Nicotero faces off with a professional wrestler on the streets of New Orleans! Plus, he asks locals questions about a man with more than 2,000 Crocs and an unorthodox cologne.

programme.title: Rickwood
programme.desc: This is the story of the oldest professional ballpark in the U.S. and the determination of its home team, the Black Barons. The voice of Rickwood tells the tale of its pivotal role in The Negro League, Black history and its legacy in American sports.

programme.title: Weathering the Storm
programme.desc: Hurricane Ian unleashed catastrophic destruction across Southwest Florida, washing entire neighborhoods to sea. As loved ones are separated and communities face unimaginable loss, extraordinary acts of courage emerge in the storm’s aftermath.

programme.title: Crimes, Cons & Capers
programme.desc: In these real-life stories of the illicit drug trade, learn how law enforcement beats the smugglers in their high-stakes game of cat and mouse.

programme.title: Best of the West
programme.desc: A look back at a KCRA show that celebrated everything the Western states had to offer.

programme.title: Lost Innocence: The Tragedy of Harmony Montgomery
programme.desc: A police investigation into a missing seven-year-old girl in Manchester, New Hampshire reveals harrowing details that shock the state. But even more disturbing is the discovery that the tragic outcome could have been avoided.

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: In 1990, an 18-year-old driving across Wisconsin to a job conference never arrives. After her van and later her body are discovered, the mystery surrounding Berit Beck’s death persists for more than two decades. What happened in that van?

programme.title: Fighting for Freedom: The United States v. Hamid Hayat
programme.desc: 14 years after he was arrested on federal terrorism charges, a Northern California man is freed after his case is overturned.

programme.title: Always Remember Your Name

programme.title: Crimes, Cons & Capers

programme.desc: (2022) As a symbol of Steelers victory, the Terrible Towel has become a sinister sign for other NFL teams. The Towel followed the Steelers from the playoffs through their second Super Bowl win.

programme.title: Maker Nation
programme.desc: A Boston jeweler with a unique medium, and kitty spaceships in Pittsburgh.

programme.title: 3, 2, 1: Blastoff
programme.desc: Tucked between a McDonald's and Wendy's, hundreds of people in the City of Pittsburgh are working to accomplish America's next mission to the moon.

programme.title: Ed & Day in The 'Burgh
programme.desc: Ed and Day kick butt, ride dirty, and make artisanal cocktails at Goodlander Cocktail Brewery in the town of Larimer.

programme.title: Eat Play Stay
programme.desc: Browse a haunted bookshop, indulge in veggie-licious vegan treats and tour the town with your very own wise man in the land of continuous Christmas.

programme.title: Chaos & Kindness
programme.desc: Justin pushes body and mind through seven brutal marathons on seven continents in seven days. From frostbite in Antarctica to his daughter joining him for the final steps in Miami, this is the most intense and inspiring challenge of his life.

programme.title: WTAE Chronicle
programme.desc: Looking back on a decade of Chronicles at WTAE and revisiting some of the most impactful stories we’ve told about Pittsburghers.

programme.title: Plate It!
programme.desc: It's not a Steelers game without the food. Chef Cory Hughes and Chef Les Murphy face off in the kitchen gridiron to see who can score the best Steelers tailgating dishes.

programme.title: Chaos & Kindness
programme.desc: Davin hits the streets in his new tree costume and attempts to jump-scare passersby. Ryan then offers them a chance to win money by answering trivia questions. Later, Justin surprises an old friend with a rare cat she has dreamt of owning for years.

programme.title: In Transit by Esquire
programme.desc: Esquire’s Dave Holmes chats with Girls5eva’s Busy Philipps. Dave and Busy play Travels and Tribulations, and Dave shares his Esquire-approved itinerary for things to do in Pittsburgh.

programme.title: Maker Nation Challenge
programme.desc: In Orlando, nothing is as it seems. For three special effects artists, it's all on the line as they compete to create an Orlando-inspired theme park character. Little do they know, things are about to get hairy.

programme.title: Blind Kitchen
programme.desc: It's the war on I-4! In a clash of the traditional and the experimental, The Milk District takes on Lake Buena Vista. Will Chef Ian Russell of Smoke & Donuts bring the heat or will Chef Norberto of El Patron smoke the competition?

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: Known as one of the first cases of the digital age, Maura Murray's mysterious disappearance continues to confound investigators and internet sleuths.

programme.title: Chaos & Kindness
programme.desc: After receiving an email from an 11 year old girl the guys jump into action to help her single mother of 4 furnish their new apartment. Justin quickly realizes they need more help than he anticipated, and now must scramble to fulfill his promise.

programme.title: Plate It Picklesburgh
programme.desc: Plate It Picklesburgh

programme.title: Finding Adventure
programme.desc: Nycid Velasquez is a young mother with crippling social anxiety. Kinga takes Nycid on a life-changing adventure from thrill riding on ATVs to taking a plunge with sharks. In the process, Nycid discovers the confidence she’s been missing.

programme.title: Heroes On Call
programme.desc: In the 1960s, Americans were dying at shocking rates due to inadequate emergency care. Enter Freedom House Ambulance Service: the first paramedics unit in the U.S., run and staffed by Black men and women. This is the origin story of EMS.

programme.title: Weathering the Storm
programme.desc: Hurricane Ian unleashed catastrophic destruction across Southwest Florida, washing entire neighborhoods to sea. As loved ones are separated and communities face unimaginable loss, extraordinary acts of courage emerge in the storm’s aftermath.

programme.title: Americas Most Delish
programme.desc: Sink your teeth into the Midwest's sweets. From the messy madness of puppy chow to kolaches to the infamous buckeye, enjoy the delicious confections that make the region a haven for dessert lovers.

programme.title: My Perfect Day
programme.desc: Chris has an unstoppable spirit, despite the challenges of Down syndrome. His resilience has propelled him to extraordinary achievements, including clinching the gold in Greece. Meaghan takes him on a whirlwind of experiences tailored just for him.

programme.title: Ed & Day in The 'Burgh
programme.desc: Ed and Day head to Bloomfield for a rousing game of dek hockey, an education in cheese, and an intro to glass art. The guys finish their day at a “slappers and bangers” party.

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: A teenager vanishes from her home one fall morning in 1999. Her body is discovered 17 days later, seven miles away. It takes more than 15 years for the heartbreaking truth to come out.

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: A Missouri community seeks answers when two young women disappear a decade apart

programme.title: Rickwood
programme.desc: This is the story of the oldest professional ballpark in the U.S. and the determination of its home team, the Black Barons. The voice of Rickwood tells the tale of its pivotal role in The Negro League, Black history and its legacy in American sports.

programme.title: Crimes, Cons & Capers
programme.desc: Escaping prison by forging the judge's signature? Biting a police officer after being arrested for check fraud? Selling stolen homes using phony papers? You won't want to miss these wild scams.

programme.title: Chaos & Kindness
programme.desc: Ryan and Justin make dreams come true when they throw a second chance prom for the community. Ryan surprises a longtime friend with a promposal, and makes Jimmy Luv a King.

programme.title: Plate It!

programme.title: Absolutely Albuquerque

programme.title: Searching for Johnny
programme.desc: (2022) The disappearance of Johnny Gosch is one of Iowa's longest running, and most notorious mysteries. In 1982, the 12-year-old paperboy in West Des Moines vanished without a trace. Forty years later, KCCI uncovers where the investigation stands.

programme.title: Crimes, Cons & Capers
programme.desc: From a 300-year-old violin valued at $6 million, to thousands of rare books, thieves will go to wild, even dangerous, lengths to snatch up irreplaceable treasures. For investigators, it's a race against the clock to recover these priceless valuables.

programme.title: Justin, Scott and Spiegel Shouldn't Be on TV: May 2026
programme.desc: We are back with an episode of Justin, Scott, and Spiegel Shouldn't be on TV, the first one of 2026! The guys kick this one off with one of several Scott-centric segments, the first of which being his attempt at lifting 20 pounds with his manhood. We then get to see how the guys' first Pilates class went. Of course, no episode is complete without a dose of Spiegel, and we take a closer look at some of his best news headlines (so far) of the year.

programme.title: My Perfect Day
programme.desc: Dedicated mom and nurse, Cleo, spends a day filled with surprises. This incredible mother has overcome obstacles no parent should have to face. Cleo and her sons cook up some quality time.

programme.title: Stitch
programme.desc: Sports create pockets of communities all across our country. For people of all ages who love the game, it's more than just exercise: it can give hope, support, inspiration and a future.

programme.title: Plate It!
programme.desc: In this battle of the grill, Chef Todd Cassell and Chef Alekka Sweeney face-off to see who can bring home the bragging rights with their Pittsburgh inspired dishes.

programme.title: Finding Adventure
programme.desc: Three years after his double amputation, Jeff in Albuquerque is ready to reconnect with nature. Kinga is there to root him on as he takes on challenging and inspiring outdoor adventures.

programme.title: Hit Me With Your Best Spot
programme.desc: With the help of notable Kansas City locals Matt Besler, Kemet Coleman, and DJs Ice Kole & Joe, we discover a silent disco aboard a KC streetcar, explore The French Market, and make ourselves at home amongst the stars at Garozzo's Italian tavern.

programme.title: Eat Play Stay
programme.desc: Embark on an out-of-this-world journey to Roswell, New Mexico! Unveil intriguing alien mysteries, order from a McDonald's with a secret menu and stay the night in a Cold War missile bunker. Don't miss this extraordinary adventure!

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: The disappearance of 16-year-old JaRay Wilson spawns rumors of human trafficking in her small town in 2012. Investigators find her body and the truth, after an unlikely informant provides new details.

programme.title: Sports Town Stories

programme.title: Local Love
programme.desc: Luke is a Kansas City bachelor who is finally ready to settle down. He’s purging the dating apps and hoping to find the perfect match. He’ll meet three blind dates as they play pickleball, get their shuffleboard on and visit a llama farm.

programme.title: KMBC Local News
programme.desc: Get the latest news and weather from KMBC.

programme.title: Maddy & Dixie Take Sactown
programme.desc: Mermaids, donuts, trains, and breaking stuff. Maddy & Dixie take a trip down memory lane in Sacramento and bring viewers to spots that remind them of childhood, with a grown-up twist.

programme.title: Rickwood
programme.desc: This is the story of the oldest professional ballpark in the U.S. and the determination of its home team, the Black Barons. The voice of Rickwood tells the tale of its pivotal role in The Negro League, Black history and its legacy in American sports.

programme.title: Crimes, Cons & Capers
programme.desc: From paying ransoms to scouring hand-drawn treasure maps, these theft victims will do anything to recover their precious property.

programme.title: Stitch
programme.desc: These stories showcase courageous Americans who came to the rescue

programme.title: One Pill Can Kill
programme.desc: Told through the voices of grieving parents, this documentary follows the real and lasting impact of the fentanyl crisis. Capturing the aftermath of sudden loss, the search for answers, and the transformation of grief into public advocacy.

programme.title: Eat Play Stay
programme.desc: Whether it’s hot chicken or gas station pad thai, the Magic City offers plenty of ways to make good things disappear.

programme.title: Completed Episodes
programme.desc: Indulge in the old-school classics that have reigned in the Midwest for decades. From Wisconsin cheese curds to Kansas City's burnt ends, enjoy these unique comfort foods that are as timeless as they are tasty.

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: Three brutal cold cases remain unsolved for decades until advances in forensic technology bring investigators closer than ever to finding answers for the victims’ families and friends.

programme.title: Eat Play Stay
programme.desc: In historic Ybor City, different cultures bring out the best in one another. Enjoy an authentic Cuban sandwich or dine at a restaurant now on its fifth generation of ownership. After chicken yoga (yes, that’s a thing!), treat yourself to a hand-rolled cigar.

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: In Milwaukee, 19-year-old Sade Robinson has gone missing, and her car is found torched. A trail of evidence across the city shocks the community and leaves her family searching for justice.

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: Detectives are baffled after a mother and daughter in an affluent family are murdered outside Louisville. A year later, a triple homicide in North Carolina sends detectives on an explosive search, linking the two cases through an unlikely suspect.

programme.title: Unbelievable
programme.desc: Brace yourself for heart-stopping heroics as rescuers leap into action! A semi-truck teeters dangerously over the Ohio River, a devastating gas explosion reduces homes to rubble, and a capsized cargo ship leaves its crew fighting for survival.

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: Three-year-old Kamille McKinney, affectionately known as "Cupcake," is abducted while playing outside a relative's Birmingham home. The ensuing search unifies the city, as residents pray she will be found alive.

programme.title: Chaos & Kindness
programme.desc: Who says you can't go home again? The team travels back to where it all began to pay back the kindness they experienced 25 years ago when they first got started.

programme.title: Boston Rob Does Beantown
programme.desc: A car or the T might be the typical way to travel, but Rob’s on a mission to explore some of the more unique modes of transport the Boston area’s got to offer.

programme.title: Disney 100
programme.desc: (2024) As Disney celebrates its 100th anniversary, and KC's Union Station prepares to host a special exhibit, we're looking at how the "house of mouse" got its start in Marceline, Missouri.

programme.title: Finding Adventure
programme.desc: Glenda Macatangay has conquered her career and being a single mom to four kids. But when it comes to being comfortable in the outdoors, she isn’t as confident. Kinga meets up with Glenda in Sacramento, CA to introduce her to the wonders of the wilderness.

programme.title: Weathering the Storm
programme.desc: Hurricane Ian unleashed catastrophic destruction across Southwest Florida, washing entire neighborhoods to sea. As loved ones are separated and communities face unimaginable loss, extraordinary acts of courage emerge in the storm’s aftermath.

programme.title: LIVE: KMBC 9 News
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the KMBC 9 News Team.

programme.title: Plate It!
programme.desc: Chef Nissa'a Stallworth and Chef Joey Hilty test their limits as they work to put a dinner twist on the most important meal of the day.

programme.title: LIVE: KMBC 9 News
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the KMBC 9 News Team.

programme.title: LIVE: Sports Sunday
programme.desc: The latest updates, scores, and analysis from the KMBC 9 Sports Team.

programme.title: On The Job
programme.desc: Brendan spruces up a home for a pair of bald eagles at a predatory bird rehabilitation center, preserves the history and legacy of one of Orlando's most infamous figures, and becomes a mixologist at the trendiest cocktail bar in town.

programme.title: The Spark
programme.desc: With a lump of clay and a vision, Manchester ceramics artist Monica Leap empowers and builds community connections through pottery.

programme.title: FIXT
programme.desc: Ted wants to live to 100 with his beloved wife beside him. But his oral health threatens his future. Kristy’s a FIXT staffer desperate to smile big on her wedding day. Will love help them face their fears?

programme.title: What's On The Menu?
programme.desc: Pittsburgh loves its food trucks. Get to know some of the owners and chefs braving the cold to bring warm treats to people all over the city.

programme.title: Local Love
programme.desc: Peter’s first love may be Taylor Swift, but he’s ready to let another person in. Who will sweep this Switfy off of his feet and into his very own fairytale?

programme.title: Boston Rob Does Beantown
programme.desc: Rob journeys back to his old stomping grounds, tastes some of the most authentic Italian food the city has to offer and tests his swing on the neighborhood's popular pickle ball courts.

programme.title: Blind Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Jessy Melo of Jessy’s Clean Meals is back for redemption. Can Chef Nikki Irving, owner of Los Jefes Comida in Taunton, show how she too, can elevate clean and fresh cooking to a higher level or will the blindfold be a game changer?

programme.title: Wait, What Happened?
programme.desc: Frank Nicotero takes to the streets to quiz people on the wildest, wackiest news stories. In "Unbelievable Adventures," contestants answer questions for cash about a 700 lb. fish, a special cross-country trip and a woman taking on a bear.

programme.title: Finding Adventure
programme.desc: DJ Jenelle “J.Belle” Vannoy is living her dream of working in radio. She worked all over the country before landing in Albuquerque. As the voice of the city but new to the area, her desire to learn more about Duke City has Jenelle ready for adventure.

programme.title: Weathering the Storm
programme.desc: On December 10, 2021, a violent tornado outbreak hit western Kentucky. It was the deadliest weather event in the history of the Commonwealth. But as families faced a bleak holiday season, hope emerged in surprising ways.

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: Single father Kendal Fenwick is the 295th homicide in Baltimore in 2015. Family and investigators wonder whether his efforts to curb drugs and violence in his neighborhood made him a target. Or is it something else entirely?

programme.title: LIVE: KMBC 9 News

programme.title: Always Remember Your Name
programme.desc: A documentary of mistaken identity, hope, love, and the power of story by two Holocaust survivors.

programme.title: Game Gone Wild: The Socker Slam Story
programme.desc: A stranger than fiction documentary that chronicles a semi-scripted extreme sporting league that premiered in 2000. Socker Slam took elements of wrestling, rugby and “butts up” shenanigans and body slammed them into a soccer drama.

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: When homesick college student Miya Marcano books a flight to south Florida for the weekend, her family is thrilled. But when the plane lands, Miya is nowhere to be found.

programme.title: Show Me Milwaukee
programme.desc: What’s the best way to experience Milwaukee? By boat! Greg gets a kayaking tour from the founder of Support Local MKE, Marleigh Petras, who shows him all the best spots to hit along the Menomonee River.

programme.title: Weathering the Storm
programme.desc: Hurricane Ian unleashed catastrophic destruction across Southwest Florida, washing entire neighborhoods to sea. As loved ones are separated and communities face unimaginable loss, extraordinary acts of courage emerge in the storm’s aftermath.

programme.title: Wait, What Happened?
programme.desc: Frank Nicotero is in Cincinnati asking locals questions about wild and wacky news stories that include some unbelievable weddings, kissing creatures and a swimming pool surprise.

programme.title: Only In
programme.desc: Where can you find a marketplace about inclusion, kids with special needs inspired by nature, and a hands-on bicycle workshop? The same place with running sausages and the Bronze Fonz.

programme.title: Sports Town Stories
programme.desc: From a popular new pro volleyball team drawing huge crowds, to a former football pro who now coaches e-sports, these Nebraskan athletes show how to seize the moment.

programme.title: Crimes, Cons & Capers
programme.desc: Siblings stealing money from a Girl Scout? A father-daughter burglary ring? In these unusual cases, family members find themselves in hot water with the justice system.

programme.title: The Freedom Center
programme.desc: Across centuries of struggle, freedom has carried many meanings, some painful, some powerful. This documentary pulls back the curtain on forgotten stories and surprising truths, leaving one question: what does freedom really look like today, and what might it become tomorrow?

programme.title: Boston Rob Does Beantown
programme.desc: Rob hits up a new wine tasting spot, gets a butt contouring treatment, knocks down some pins and then hits the ice at Warrior Ice Arena.

programme.title: Blind Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Aaron Poret of Bywater American Bistro goes head to head with Chef Jas Rogers, Owner and Creator of Saucex Dat. Will fried dough foil Chef Poret’s chances of success, or will he rise to Chef Rogers’ challenge?

programme.title: Local Love
programme.desc: Sam from Salem is ready to fall under someone's spell. Which bachelor will charm his way into her heart?

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: A Missouri community seeks answers when two young women disappear a decade apart

programme.title: Plate It!
programme.desc: Pop the champagne: we’re getting fancy! Will Chef Polo Adamo incorporate the caviar more creatively than Chef Dennis Sydnor? It's anybody's gourmet guess.

programme.title: Finding Adventure
programme.desc: Kinga is in Manchester, NH, to meet Matt Krajcik, a 26-year-old curriculum developer who survived a trying childhood. Now he is looking to reconnect to the nature around him that provided respite when he was young.

programme.title: Stitch
programme.desc: Explore how designers blend heritage and innovation in fashion. From cultural celebrations to empowering future generations, their creative visions sew a new tomorrow.

programme.title: The Roller Coaster Wars That Birthed a Beast
programme.desc: In 2006, two dozen roller coaster riders landed in the hospital after a freak malfunction in Ohio. This is the story of Kings Island: how its thrilling – and sometimes nail-biting – history spawned one of America’s greatest amusement parks.

programme.title: Show Me Milwaukee
programme.desc: Join fashion icon Christopher DeAngelo Gilbert as he takes Greg on a creative adventure. Together they dance, get styled at a unique tailor and hat maker, create a memorable dinner, and improv with Milwaukee's next generation of actors.

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: The brutal murders of two trans women of color just 48 hours apart in New Orleans unsettle the community, leaving many wondering if the cases are connected. Detectives uncover clues, yet justice remains elusive.

programme.title: Eat Play Stay
programme.desc: Discover where they serve the best omelets on the planet in ‘Biggest Little City in the World.’ Meet an unlikely taxidermist, open a secret door to a room filled with booze, and sample the cuisine of other cultures by eating hot dogs.

programme.title: Free Spirit
programme.desc: Kate learns how to play a Germantown street game, gets up close and personal with handcrafted, hand-charred bourbon barrels, and learns to fall back in love with whiskey – and natural wine.

programme.title: Plate It!
programme.desc: Chefs Sean Guy and Amy Hessel compete in this seafood showdown, the ultimate Battle of the Bay. Who will walk away with the title Captain of the Chesapeake?

programme.title: Weathering the Storm
programme.desc: Hurricane Ian unleashed catastrophic destruction across Southwest Florida, washing entire neighborhoods to sea. As loved ones are separated and communities face unimaginable loss, extraordinary acts of courage emerge in the storm’s aftermath.

programme.title: Blind Kitchen
programme.desc: Big City Chef Hish Hassan from Chickadees in Boston takes on Small town Chef J. Huntington Chase of The Rabbit Hole in Worthington in an explosion of flavors and techniques. Who will prevail in this Urban vs Rural showdown?

programme.title: Finding Adventure
programme.desc: When it comes to nature, Maddie Carlin is a self-proclaimed Debbie Downer. Kinga is in Milwaukee to convince her to give nature another chance. They’re traveling across the state, looking for an adventure to inspire Maddie to get off the couch!

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: When body parts wash ashore in the Gulf Coast over several days in 2012, investigators must identify the victim before they can find the killers. Even then, justice is elusive.

programme.title: Fighting for Freedom: The United States v. Hamid Hayat
programme.desc: 14 years after he was arrested on federal terrorism charges, a Northern California man is freed after his case is overturned.

programme.title: Ed & Day in The 'Burgh
programme.desc: Ed and Day travel to Homewood to see if they have what it takes to be urban beekeepers, elite barbers, and African dancers and drummers.

programme.title: The Spark
programme.desc: From Naval helicopter pilot to fantasy costume designer, Zan Campbell’s life has been an adventure. Learn about his journey to find purpose and inspire joy in others.

programme.title: Show Me Milwaukee
programme.desc: KISS-FM radio host, Alley Faith joins Greg to explore places that put Milwaukee on the map. They visit the Harley Davidson Museum, a unique cheese creamery and ring the bell with Mayor Cavalier Johnson atop what was once the world's tallest building.

programme.title: Eat Play Stay
programme.desc: Pour wine out of a wall, solve a murder over dinner and make friends at one of the many music venues in this cozy PA county.

programme.title: Finding Adventure
programme.desc: Soon-to-be-married David and Justin both work long hours in education. When the school year is over, they go abroad for adventure. Kinga shows them there is plenty to do in their own backyard in Cincinnati.

programme.title: Good Deed DIY
programme.desc: Monica teams up with Sac Fire to give a Fire Captain the ultimate office makeover. Can they transform the sad pad into a stress-free sanctuary for the team?

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: After a snowy day in Western Pennsylvania, a horrifying discovery leads investigators down a heartbreaking and twisted path. They'll seek swift justice for an innocent victim deceived.

programme.title: Completed Episodes
programme.desc: Indulge in the old-school classics that have reigned in the Midwest for decades. From Wisconsin cheese curds to Kansas City's burnt ends, enjoy these unique comfort foods that are as timeless as they are tasty.

programme.title: Searching for Johnny
programme.desc: (2022) The disappearance of Johnny Gosch is one of Iowa's longest running, and most notorious mysteries. In 1982, the 12-year-old paperboy in West Des Moines vanished without a trace. Forty years later, KCCI uncovers where the investigation stands.

programme.title: The Middle Child
programme.desc: In the 1980s, a serial killer buried a woman and three girls in the woods of New Hampshire. One of those girls is still unidentified. WMUR joins the investigators who promise they won’t give up until her name is restored.

programme.title: My Perfect Day
programme.desc: Kar is a selfless soul known for his unwavering commitment to the unhoused in his community. It's Kar's turn to have some good old-fashioned fun.

programme.title: Weathering the Storm
programme.desc: Hurricane Ian unleashed catastrophic destruction across Southwest Florida, washing entire neighborhoods to sea. As loved ones are separated and communities face unimaginable loss, extraordinary acts of courage emerge in the storm’s aftermath.
